visa_types.name: OCI Miscellaneous Services

Fees and Charges

[inr > gbr > en > OCI Services > OCI Miscellaneous Services: table]

Click Here to Download the detailed Checklist
visa_types.overview:
Overview
OCI Miscellaneous Services are availed for updating or modifying details in an existing OCI card, such as changes in personal particulars or updating a new passport number. Applicants seeking to update their passport details in the OCI records may utilize the gratis (free) online service, provided the request is submitted within 90 days of issuance of the new passport. If this 90-day window has elapsed, the applicant will be required to submit a physical OCI Miscellaneous application through the prescribed process.
Important Note - In cases where the OCI card is damaged and the applicant also intends to update or modify personal particulars, the applicant must apply under OCI Miscellaneous Services. Selecting the “Damaged OCI” category alone will not permit any changes to personal details, and therefore the Miscellaneous Service option must be chosen to process both requests together.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
OCI Miscellaneous Services are availed for updating or modifying details in an existing OCI card, such as changes in personal particulars or updating a new passport number. Applicants seeking to update their passport details in the OCI records may utilize the gratis (free) online service, provided the request is submitted within 90 days of issuance of the new passport. If this 90-day window has elapsed, the applicant will be required to submit a physical OCI Miscellaneous application through the prescribed process.
Important Note - In cases where the OCI card is damaged and the applicant also intends to update or modify personal particulars, the applicant must apply under OCI Miscellaneous Services. Selecting the “Damaged OCI” category alone will not permit any changes to personal details, and therefore the Miscellaneous Service option must be chosen to process both requests together.

Overview
Check your eligibility before you start your registration.
To avail OCI in lieu of Damaged/Lost OCI card Service, you must be an existing OCI Card Holder and should select “OCI Miscellaneous services on Govt. Portal
In case of urgent travel, applicants may apply for appropriate visa. In case of urgent travel, applicants may apply for appropriate visa.

visa_types.name: New OCI Registration
visa_types.documents_required:
Fees and Charges

[inr > gbr > en > OCI Services > New OCI Registration: table]

Click Here to Download the detailed Checklist
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Apply for OCI Services
While applying for an OCI, it is mandatory for former Indian nationals to provide Certificate of Renunciation/Surrender Certificate of Indian citizenship. If you do not have the Renunciation Certificate/Surrender Certificate, please apply for it first and after getting the renunciation certificate, please apply for OCI.
Eligibility for registration as OCI Cardholder:
- Following categories of foreign nationals are eligible for registration as Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) Cardholder:
- Any person of full age and capacity:
- who is a citizen of another country, but was a citizen of India at the time of, or at any time after the commencement of the Constitution i.e. 26.01.1950; or
- who is a citizen of another country, but was eligible to become a citizen of India at the time of the commencement of the Constitution i.e. on 26.01.1950; or
- who is a citizen of another country, but belonged to a territory that became part of India after 15.08.1947; or
- who is a child or a grandchild or a great grandchild of such a citizen;
- A person, who is a minor child of a person mentioned in (a) above;
- A person, who is a minor child, and whose both parents are citizens of India or one of the parents is a citizen of India;
- Spouse of foreign origin of a citizen of India or spouse of foreign origin of an Overseas Citizen of India Cardholder registered under section 7A of the Citizenship Act, 1955 and whose marriage has been registered and subsisted for a continuous period of not less than two years immediately preceding the presentation of the application.
- Following categories of foreign nationals are NOT eligible for registration as Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) Cardholder:
- Who or either of whose parents or grandparents or great grandparents is or had been a citizen of Pakistan, Bangladesh or such other country as the Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, specify.
- Foreign Military/Police personnel either serving or retired.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Apply for OCI Services
While applying for an OCI, it is mandatory for former Indian nationals to provide Certificate of Renunciation/Surrender Certificate of Indian citizenship. If you do not have the Renunciation Certificate/Surrender Certificate, please apply for it first and after getting the renunciation certificate, please apply for OCI.
Eligibility for registration as OCI Cardholder:
- Following categories of foreign nationals are eligible for registration as Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) Cardholder:
- Any person of full age and capacity:
- who is a citizen of another country, but was a citizen of India at the time of, or at any time after the commencement of the Constitution i.e. 26.01.1950; or
- who is a citizen of another country, but was eligible to become a citizen of India at the time of the commencement of the Constitution i.e. on 26.01.1950; or
- who is a citizen of another country, but belonged to a territory that became part of India after 15.08.1947; or
- who is a child or a grandchild or a great grandchild of such a citizen;
- A person, who is a minor child of a person mentioned in (a) above;
- A person, who is a minor child, and whose both parents are citizens of India or one of the parents is a citizen of India;
- Spouse of foreign origin of a citizen of India or spouse of foreign origin of an Overseas Citizen of India Cardholder registered under section 7A of the Citizenship Act, 1955 and whose marriage has been registered and subsisted for a continuous period of not less than two years immediately preceding the presentation of the application.
- Following categories of foreign nationals are NOT eligible for registration as Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) Cardholder:
- Who or either of whose parents or grandparents or great grandparents is or had been a citizen of Pakistan, Bangladesh or such other country as the Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, specify.
- Foreign Military/Police personnel either serving or retired.

visa_types.name: Renunciation of OCI Card
visa_types.documents_required:
Fees and Charges

[inr > gbr > en > OCI Services > Renunciation of OCI Card: Table]

Click Here to Download the detailed Checklist
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Renunciation of OCI Card is required when an OCI cardholder wishes to formally surrender or cancel their OCI status, including in cases where the OCI was issued in error, held in duplicate, or is no longer required. Applicants must apply under the prescribed renunciation process to ensure that the OCI records are officially cancelled in accordance with applicable regulations.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Renunciation of OCI Card is required when an OCI cardholder wishes to formally surrender or cancel their OCI status, including in cases where the OCI was issued in error, held in duplicate, or is no longer required. Applicants must apply under the prescribed renunciation process to ensure that the OCI records are officially cancelled in accordance with applicable regulations.
